def: postpn. (Braj.) = se, and soṅ, qq.v. The sixth letter of the Hindūstānī alphabet (the fourth of the Arabic) occurs only in words borrowed from the Arabic. Aise samjhābat maiṅ bāre balam ko, jaise koī paṛhavat sūā, Rāt din dārī-jār! khele juā . Farrukh. Wom. song. My spouse! I counsel you each day, Just as a parrot teach one may, The wretch! he gambles night and day. 3. Mūṅhbaṭvā sā, nāk suā sī . Prov. The mouth as small as purse closed up, a nose like parrot's beaked.

def: n. m. سأل asked. 1. Query (استفسار 1). Savāl dīgar, javāb dīgar . P. Prov. Cross purposes and crooked answers. 2. Application; petition; request. 3. Begging; asking; demand; requisition. Faqīr kī sūrat savāl hai . Prov. The beggar's aspect is a silent appeal for alms. 4. A sum; problem. savāl-i-ishārī, yā hidāyatī , A leading question. savāl banānā , v. a. To set questions. savāl-i-tardīd, yā jirah , n. A cross-question; cross-examination. savāl javāb . To set a problem or question for solution. savāl-i-zurūrī , A material question. savāl k. , v. a. 1. To question; ask; interrogate. 2. To examine (a witness). 3. ( savāl ḍālnā ) To ask; beg; request. savālāt-i-ibtidāī , Preliminary question. savālāt-i-imtehān , Examination questions. savālāt-i-tahrīrī . Written questions. savālāt-i-hal-talab . n. m. S. स्वार्थ from स्व own, अर्थ purpose. 1. Self-interest ( khud-garzī ). 1. Sur, nar, munī kī yehī rītī. Swāruth lāë karen sab prītī . Rāmāyan. With gods, and man, and sage, the rule's the same, All love is selfishness whate'er its name. 2. Sāīṅ! is sansār meṅ suārath kā behvār, Jab lag paisā gāṅṭh kā jab lag uskā yār . Kunḍalya. Lord! in this world all seek their selfish ends, The man with money is the man with friends. 3. Arebhāī! pīse dhele ke suārath māṅ biyāh kāj bigṛe saë . Rus. My dear fellow! dont spoil the wedding feast for the sake of a pice or so. 2. Wish; desire; pleasure. 3. Object; purpose; aim. Jāko jāṅ suārath sadhe, soī tāhe suhāt, Chor na piyārī chāṅdnī jaisī kārī rāt . Prov. Whate'er one's ends promotes to him seems bright, No moon delights the thief as doth dark night. suārath k. , v. a. 1. To bring into use; make use of. 2. To render useful or beneficial. Khudā sab kī mehnat suārath kartā hai, akārath nahīṅ kartā . Prov. God giveth to every man the fruits of his labour, he rendereth it not vain. suārath lagnā , v. n. To be spent usefully. Biṭyā! pahilo māl jau dharo hato tau ī bakhat meṅ sab suārath lago . Rus. Farrukh. Daughter! this money saved up in days gone by has been of use this day. (For her marriage).

more: intj. 1. Heaven be praised! 1. Maulvī. Subhān terī qudrat! Glory to the Author of nature! Green grocer. Haldī, mirach, adrak! Turmeric, chillies, ginger. Butcher. Sirī, pāë ḍhak rakh! The head and trotters cover! (The cry of the black partridge or francolin differently interpreted by different castes). 2. Sab mast ho rahe haiṅ pahchān terī qudrat! Tītar pukārte haiṅ 'subhān terī qudrat! ' Nazīr. Creative power displayed, with rapture all intoxicate, The partridge gladsome sings 'all praise to God almighty great!' 2. Good gracious! Baṛe miyāṅ so baṛe mīyāṅ chhoṭe miyāṅ subhān Allāh! Prov. The father, well, he is a great man; but the son, good God! 3. Bravo! well done!
s. m. Praising, glorifying (God). سبحان الله subʼḥāna-llāh, interj. O God! O holy God! Good God! Ha! سبحانه subʼḥāna-hu, Praise to him!
subs: Far be it from God! Holy God! Good God! God be praised! wonderful! strange! bravo! well-done!

Wealth and a hall to catch one quality in common own, One moment to your hand it comes, the next from you it's gone. 2. Valekin ye khūbāṅ kā dekhā subhāo, Ki bigṛe se dūnā ho unkā banāo . Mīr Hasan. Of beauteous belles this I have seen and known, When angry they seem twice as beauteous grown. 3. Jāko paṛo subhāo jāëgo jiu se, Nīm na mīṭhā hoë sīṅ ch guṛ ghiu se . Prov. The bitter nīm not sweet becomes with ghī and sugar fed, Whate'er your nature is 'twill stick to you till you are dead. 4. Man, motī aur dūdh ras, inkā ye hī subhāo, Phāṭe se juṛte nahīṅ, koṭan karo upāo . Dohā. A heart, and pearl, and milk in common have this quality, Parted they never join though million schemes contrived there be. 2. Habit; custom. Rasyā! merī bāt ko buro na mā…
s. m. Good disposition, nature, natural state, property, disposition. adj. Well-disposed, of good quality. See سوبهاو.

more: adj. Good-looking; handsome ( khūb-sūrat ). sudarshan , n. m. 1. See sukh-darshan . 2. The rose-apple; Jambosa vulgaris. sudarshan-chakr , n. m. The discus of Vishnu .
subs: s.m. The discus of Vishṇu or Krishṇa ( = su-darśan).

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A good slave or servant; — name of an ancient Hindū king (at whose court both Vasishṭa and Viśvamitra appear to have acted as family priests).
(su+daśā, qq.v.), Good condition or circumstances; fortunate or happy lot; well-being, prosperity.
adj. = سڐول su-ḍaul, q.v. adj. Well-formed, well-shaped, symmetrical, elegant, graceful, handsome
subs: s.f. Gracefulness of bodily shape or form, handsomeness.

def: adj. Well-formed, well-shaped, symmetrical, elegant, graceful, handsome
more: adj. Well-shaped, graceful. سڐول ديـہي su-ḍaul deh-ī, s. f. Gracefulness of bodily shape, handsomeness.
subs: s.f. Gracefulness of bodily shape or form, handsomeness.

def: adj. S. सु good, H. ḍhāl shape. See ṭhīk , 10, 11. su-ḍaul biddiyā , n. f. Crystallography.
s.f. Excellence of shape or form, elegance, gracefulness, prettiness, handsomeness.

s.m. Good gift, auspicious gift; a special gift given on particular solemn occasions (e.g. a gift to a student at his investiture with the sacred thread, a nuptial present, &c.); — one who makes the above present (e.g. a mother, father, husband, &c.).

ety: for A. سدة suddat, v.n. fr. سدّ 'to stop up, to obstruct'
def: s.m. An obstruction in the nose, coryza; (in Med.) any obstruction in the body; scybala; — a vestibule, porch; threshold.
more: n. m. 1. ( Med .) An obstruction. 2. Hardened excrement; scybala .

n. f. S. सुधी See chit . 1. Memory; remembrance. 2. Sensation; perception; consciousness. 3. Attention. 4. Care; regard. Mujh be-dhaṅgī meṅ ḍhaṅg nahīṅ, ghar meṅ bairan sās, Pī piyāre se ras nahīṅ, kaise hoë ghar-bās, Sās batāve bāorī, nanad lagāve dokh, Ke to pīyāsudh karo, nahīṅ mero gatī na mokh ! Wom. Untutored I no skill possess, my mother-in-law my foe, No pleasure with my spouse I have, how in the house live so? My mother-in-law calls me a fool, my sister-in-law but blames, Look on me, husband, whom else earth regards, nor Heaven claims! 5. News; intelligence. 1. Barkhā bigat, sarad ritu āī, sudhi na tāt, Sītā kī pāī . Rāmāyan. 2. Na khāne ḳī sudh, aur na pīne kā hosh, Bharā dil meṅ uske muhabbat kā josh . Mīr Hasan. sudh-budh , n. f. See sudh . 1. Right senses; sense; common sense; sound mind. 2. Discrimination; discretion; prudence. 3. Consciousness; presence of mind. sudh-budh …

adj. S. सिध् 1. Effected; accomplished; fulfilled; perfected. 2. Perfect; sanctified; holy. 3. Sound; valid ( ṭhīk , 14). 4. Right; lawful ( bajā , 1). siddh , n. m. 1. A saint; a holy man; one who has subjected to his will the eight siddhīs . 2. Demigods inhabiting Indra's heaven. siddh k. , v. a. 1. To accomplish; complete; finish. 2. To perfect; sanctify. 3. To bring under subjection by magic. 4. To set out; start. Achchhā bhāī, siddh karo! sidh h. , v. n. 1. To be accomplished. 2. To be perfect.
(from سدّ) s. m. 1. (in medicine) An obstruction. 2. Scybala. 3. Stoppage of the nose, coryza. 4. A threshold.
